svg.title: LinkedIn Object Storage Analysis — S3 vs Azure Blob
svg.desc: Feature-by-feature breakdown of LinkedIn data mapped to hot/cool/archive tiers across AWS S3 and Azure Blob Storage, with lifecycle rule recommendations.
svg.text: Min object size for IA: 128 KB
Winner: Azure Blob — Hot/Cool/Archive naming maps 1-to-1 to feature requirement, simpler config
Feature 2 — Frequent reads & append (profile views, connect requests)
S3 Object Append not native — use multipart
or write new object per event (costly at scale)
S3-Standard for hot, lifecycle to IA at 90d
Append Blob type — designed for log append
Each AppendBlock call adds a segment
Hot tier → Cool at 90d via lifecycle policy
Winner: Azure Blob — Append Blob is a native blob type; S3 has no equivalent, requires workarounds
Tie — S3 Glacier Instant wins on restore speed; Azure Archive wins on lifecycle label clarity

g.text: More archive sub-tiers for cost tuning

svg.text: Verdict: Azure Blob Storage for this LinkedIn scenario
Append Blob + native tier naming eliminates custom logic. Choose S3 if you need sub-hour archive restores.
All three features can be implemented on both platforms — Azure wins on native API alignment
